user_query:
I'm an architect hosting guests in my central Tirana studio, and I want to give them a precise, design-minded mini-guide while keeping my co-host in the loop. Using the inputs below, please do three things and save everything under the specified paths:

1) Build and run a local script to compute walkable cultural highlights:
- Create scripts/build_poi_summary.py that reads input/apartment.json and input/nearby_pois.csv, computes great-circle (Haversine) distance in meters from the apartment to each POI, then filters POIs to those within 1,500 meters inclusive.
- Assume walking_speed_m_per_min = 70. For each included POI, compute walk_time_min = ceil(distance_m / 70).
- Sort included POIs by distance ascending.
- Write output/poi_metrics.json with this structure:
  {
    "apartment": { "name": string, "coordinates": [latitude, longitude] },
    "parameters": { "max_distance_m": 1500, "walking_speed_m_per_min": 70 },
    "included_pois": [ { "name": string, "category": string, "distance_m": integer, "walk_time_min": integer, "description": string } ],
    "by_category": { "<category>": integer, ... },
    "top5_nearest": [ "POI name", ... up to 5 items ]
  }
- Run from the command line and capture a concise run log. Use a command like:
  python scripts/build_poi_summary.py input/apartment.json input/nearby_pois.csv --out output/poi_metrics.json > output/run.log 2>&1
- The script should print a one-line summary to stdout (captured in output/run.log) in the form: "included=<N> categories=<C> nearest=<NameOfNearest>" where <N> is number of included POIs, <C> is number of distinct categories, and <NameOfNearest> is the name of the nearest POI.

2) Compose a guest-facing mini-guide using the computed data:
- Create output/guest_guide.md with the following sections:
  - Welcome & Design Note: a short paragraph from an architect’s perspective.
  - 48-Hour Itinerary: two subsections (Day 1 and Day 2). Reference at least 6 POIs from output/poi_metrics.json, listing each stop with its name, category, and walking time in minutes drawn from the JSON.
  - Cultural Context (120–200 words): a compact, original overview of Tirana’s recent history and urban fabric; no citations or external quotes needed.
  - Data Appendix: a table or list of all included POIs with their categories, distance_m, and walk_time_min exactly as in output/poi_metrics.json.
- Any distances/times cited must match the values in output/poi_metrics.json.

3) Draft two short messages:
- Create output/guest_message.txt: a friendly 120–180 word message to my next guest (name: Elena). Mention the path to the guide (output/guest_guide.md) and highlight the top 3 nearest POIs by name and walking time taken directly from output/poi_metrics.json.
- Create output/cohost_update.md: a concise status update to my co-host (name: Ardian) summarizing: (a) number of included POIs, (b) counts by category as listed in the by_category map, (c) the top5_nearest list, and (d) the exact command you used to run the script.

Inputs:
- input/apartment.json and input/nearby_pois.csv are provided below.

Deliverables to produce:
- output/poi_metrics.json (derived data for included POIs and aggregates)
- output/run.log (captured command-line execution and summary line)
- output/guest_guide.md (architect-curated 48-hour guide with data appendix)
- output/guest_message.txt (guest message for Elena)
- output/cohost_update.md (status summary for Ardian)

All figures in the guide and messages must be consistent with output/poi_metrics.json. Please ensure the deliverables can be verified locally without internet use. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
